mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, forcing immediate equity repricing and negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 50% of cases, invalidating the supply constraint mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Falling inflation combined with stable or rising real GDP growth creates a favorable macro environment where central banks can maintain accommodative policy, boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of declining CPIAUCSL and rising GDPC1 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and reduced corporate profitability expectations, systematically generating negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS declines remains below -0.02, or 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of payroll contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 1.5% signals strong consumer demand that immediately lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where RSAFS growth surpasses the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a period of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns as investors price in lower profitability.
failure_condition: Concurrent increases in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 55% of historical observations, refuting the margin compression hypothesis.
